## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On February 22, 1997, a massive, highly sensational scientific breakthrough was officially announced to the global public in Roslin, Scotland. Scientists at the Roslin Institute successfully cloned a massive, female domestic sheep named "Dolly." She was the absolute, very first mammal to be successfully cloned from an adult somatic cell. The massive, groundbreaking achievement instantly triggered a massive, highly polarized global debate regarding the ethics, legality, and terrifying potential of human cloning.

On February 22, 1980, the massive, highly improbable "Miracle on Ice" occurred during the Winter Olympics in Lake Placid, New York. The United States national men's ice hockey team, heavily composed of amateur and collegiate players, aggressively and shockingly defeated the massive, highly favored, and seemingly invincible professional team from the Soviet Union. The massive upset provided a massive, incredible psychological boost to the United States during the height of the Cold War.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On February 22, 1944, Kasturba Gandhi, the massive, deeply devoted wife of Mahatma Gandhi and a massive, fierce independence activist in her own right, passed away. She died while under strict, massive British house arrest alongside her husband at the Aga Khan Palace in Pune. Her massive, highly steadfast participation in numerous massive Satyagraha movements and her tragic death in British custody transformed her into a massive, enduring symbol of sacrifice and unwavering national commitment.

On February 22, 1958, Maulana Abul Kalam Azad, the massive, towering intellectual, Islamic scholar, and senior leader of the Indian independence movement, passed away in Delhi. He fiercely, massively opposed the Partition of India along religious lines. Serving as the very first Minister of Education in independent India, he heavily laid the massive, foundational architecture for the nation's modern educational system, heavily championing free primary education and establishing massive institutions like the IITs.
